item.title: Japan fast-tracks nuclear restart as Hormuz closure triggers energy emergency
item.description: Japan's lower house passed emergency nuclear restart legislation on an accelerated timeline after Hormuz tanker traffic dropped 91%, with South Korea introducing a parallel Energy Security Emergency Act to bring three mothballed reactors back online. Germany signaled its first strategic nuclear exit review since the 2023 shutdown; four countries have requested expedited IAEA safety reviews. Uranium spot hit /lb — up 18%, a third consecutive record week — with TEPCO's Kashiwazaki-Kariwa Unit 6 already back on the transmission grid since February 16.

item.title: Vera Rubin NVL144 packaging delayed 3-6 weeks as Gulf disruption hits TSMC supply chain
item.description: CoWoS-S packaging lead times have stretched from 8-10 weeks pre-war to 16-20 weeks, driven by Gulf shipping insurance clauses and Taiwan Strait risk premiums — threatening H2 2026 Vera Rubin delivery timelines for MSFT, Oracle, and AWS. First NVL144 racks have landed at Oracle Chicago (non-Gulf routing); Microsoft's Warsaw Azure AI region is live with 1,200 Vera Rubin GPUs. Groq cut inference pricing 60% ahead of the LPU-Vera CPX integration targeting Q1 2027, with European AI infrastructure capex up 42% in four weeks as hyperscalers diversify away from Gulf-adjacent regions.

item.title: IDF Khiam advance stalls at 860m as Radwan deploys tunnel network
item.description: Golani Brigade pushed 60 meters overnight to the 860-meter line, but IDF engineers blasting the first tunnel junction at Khiam's southeast edge triggered a Radwan repositioning into the deep urban core, effectively halting the advance. Operation Northern Shield II stands at 9 IDF KIA after 9 days; Hezbollah launched a 60-drone swarm from the Bekaa (52 intercepted, 8 hit open fields). IDF command is still saying "days not weeks" to the city center, but the tunnel network makes that optimistic.

item.title: DOJ claims "plenary" war power, rejecting 60-day congressional clock
item.description: A leaked DOJ OLC memo asserts Trump holds plenary war authority over Operation Epic Fury, explicitly rejecting the War Powers Act's 60-day clock — galvanizing opposition ahead of the April 2 House floor vote. Forty-seven constitutional scholars from Harvard, Yale, and Stanford issued a joint letter condemning the claim; Rand Paul delivered a two-hour floor speech calling it "the day the War Powers Act dies." The House whip count sits at 219-216 in favor of the resolution, with three Republicans still leaning no and Johnson working the phones.

item.title: Iran signals flexibility on Hezbollah clause as Oman deadline looms
item.description: Iran's Pezeshkian faction told Oman intermediaries that a "Litani buffer zone" framing — distinct from full Hezbollah withdrawal — "merits further study," the first crack in the Hezbollah deadlock after four days of proximity talks in Muscat. The March 31 deadline has been extended to April 1 06:00 UTC after Sultan Haitham called Rubio directly. Sequencing remains the core sticking point: Iran wants a Hormuz freeze simultaneous with any pause; the US wants it first as a confidence-builder.
